_comment: Tiny Decks — Fundraising Pitch template. DEMO/synthetic data only (Northwind Holdings). Round terms are ILLUSTRATIVE.
title: Northwind Holdings — Fundraising Pitch (DEMO)

slides.title: An Invitation to Compound
slides.subtitle: A founder-led holding company acquiring profitable internet businesses and holding them forever.

slides.title: Why Now
slides.lead: The market for durable, profitable internet businesses is large, fragmented, and mispriced.
bullets: A fragmented long tail. Thousands of bootstrapped software and creator-economy businesses do $2–20M in revenue with no natural buyer and no succession plan.
Profit over hype. We buy companies that already make money — positive Adj. EBITDA from day one, no turnaround risk, no path-to-profitability story.
Permanent capital advantage. Unlike PE, we never have to sell. Founders trust us with their life's work because we hold forever.
Compounding flywheel. Cash from the portfolio funds the next acquisition, which funds the next — a self-reinforcing engine that strengthens with scale.
Proven operating playbook. Six acquisitions in, our model — light-touch ownership, shared services, founder autonomy — is repeatable across verticals.
slides.footnote: Synthetic demonstration data. Northwind Holdings is illustrative and not a real entity.

slides.title: Where We Stand
commentary: $156M LTM revenue across six wholly-owned businesses, up 18% year over year on organic growth and two acquisitions.
$31M Adj. EBITDA at a 27% margin — every company in the portfolio is profitable and self-funding.
Disciplined cash conversion means the portfolio funds most of its own growth, with external capital reserved for acquisitions.
slides.footnote: DEMO figures. LTM = last twelve months. Adj. EBITDA is pre-head-office and pro forma for acquisitions.

slides.title: Revenue by Company
commentary: No single point of failure. Our largest company is ~27% of revenue; the portfolio is diversified across SaaS, marketplaces, and creator tools.
Acme and Initech anchor the base with durable subscription revenue, while Globex and Hooli are the fastest growers.
slides.note: DEMO — LTM revenue contribution by company ($000s).

slides.title: Adj. EBITDA Growth
commentary: 5x Adj. EBITDA in five years — from $6.2M to $31M, a ~38% compound annual growth rate.
Each cohort compounds. Acquisitions land profitable, then grow under our ownership and add to the base.

slides.title: The Round & Use of Proceeds
commentary: Acquisitions — 65% ($26M). Fund three to four new platform acquisitions over the next 24 months at our proven entry multiples.
Growth — 20% ($8M). Reinvest in the existing portfolio: hiring, product, and bolt-on tuck-ins.
Balance sheet — 15% ($6M). Pay down acquisition debt and hold a reserve to act quickly when the right business comes up.
slides.footnote: ILLUSTRATIVE round terms — for discussion only, not an offer. All figures synthetic (DEMO).

slides.title: Let's build together.
slides.subtitle: We're inviting a small group of aligned, long-term partners to compound alongside us.
slides.contact: Office of the CEO · Northwind Holdings (DEMO) · invest@northwind.example
